pattern: Characters can move freely, so the strings can all be made equal exactly when every letter's total count across all words is divisible by the number of words.
hints: Since any character can be moved anywhere, only the global frequency of each letter matters, not which word it starts in.
To split into k identical strings, each letter's total occurrences must divide evenly into k equal shares.
Count every letter over all words and return true only if each count is a multiple of the number of words.

edge_inputs.description: three a's cannot split across two words
counts divisible for one letter but not another
letter count not divisible by word count

stress.description: random lowercase words of varying counts
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    k = rng.randint(1, 60)
    words = []
    for _ in range(k):
        L = rng.randint(1, 12)
        words.append(''.join(rng.choice('abcde') for _ in range(L)))
    return [words]

stress.description: construct guaranteed-true cases by repeating one base string
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    k = rng.randint(1, 30)
    base = ''.join(rng.choice('abc') for _ in range(rng.randint(1, 8)))
    words = [base] * k
    # shuffle characters between two words sometimes to vary distribution
    if k >= 2 and len(base) >= 1:
        words[0] = words[0] + 'a'
        words[1] = words[1][1:] if len(words[1]) > 0 else words[1]
    return [words]
stress.note: mix of true and false from perturbation
